front: Work: compiled by the fictional 'Victor Eremita'; two halves by 'A' and Judge Wilhelm portray the aesthetic and ethical life views
Work: a section compares aesthetes' constant change of activity to avoid boredom with a pastoral practice
Work: in 'The Seducer's Diary,' Johannes befriends a boy named Edward to get closer to a woman named Cordelia
Work: opens with the 'Diapsalmata' section, which cites Spinoza saying 'I am continually aeterno modo'
Work: its essay 'The Tragic in Ancient Drama Reflected in the Tragic in Modern Drama' criticizes Hegel's reading of Antigone
Work: its essay 'The Immediate Erotic Stages' analyzes Don Giovanni and criticizes Hegelian aesthetics
Work: popularized the 'leap of faith' metaphor in a discussion of the sacrifice of Isaac
Concept: in Fear and Trembling, a 'Knight of Faith' makes this type of 'suspension of the ethical,' contrasted with 'infinite resignation'
Work: compares the ethical dilemmas of Agamemnon and Abraham
Work: written under pseudonym 'Johannes Climacus,' this sequel to Philosophical Fragments argues 'subjectivity is truth' and distinguishes Religiousness A and B
Work: its author 'revokes' the whole thing after 500+ pages; calls the failure to give existential advice 'the scandal of modern philosophy'
Work: illustrates subjectivity with an escaped mental patient who says 'Bang, the world is round!' to avoid detection
Work: adds the religious mode to the aesthetic and ethical, completing three 'stages'; includes a banquet speech similar to Plato's Symposium
work: Stages on Life's Way and the Three Modes

front: Work: master's thesis analyzing irony 'with Continual Reference to Socrates,' defining it as 'infinite absolute negativity'
Philosopher: journals reference the 'Great Earthquake' of discovering his father once cursed God, blamed for the deaths of five siblings
Philosopher: Karl Barth quoted this thinker's formulation of the 'infinite qualitative distinction' between God and Man
Philosopher: attacked established Christianity ('Christendom') in pamphlets comparing churches to disease-ridden hospitals
Philosopher: 'From the Papers of One Still Living' reviews Hans Christian Andersen, accusing him of lacking 'any philosophy of life'
Philosopher: processed a breakup with Regine Olsen through a book titled from Psalm 55
